common.title: Financial Overview
common.prototype_note: This is a local-only prototype. Credentials are sent directly to the local backend.
common.demo_banner: Demo mode — sample data only. No backend or bank data is used.
common.maintenance_desc: System maintenance tools and database management.
common.sidebar_placeholder: Sidebar content available in Scrape view
common.server_unreachable_hint: The backend is not responding. Restart the server or check your connection.
common.auto_detection_hint: Leave blank to attempt automatic detection from file content
common.automation_desc: Schedule and trigger automated scraper runs
common.error_occurred: An error occurred. Please try again.

scheduler.title: Scheduler
scheduler.run_time_desc: Time of day in the server's local timezone.
scheduler.interval_anchor_desc: Runs on this date, then every N days after.
scheduler.custom_cron_desc: Five fields: minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week (same as standard cron).
scheduler.insight_rules_timer_desc: Re-run insight rules on a schedule using your current transaction data—no bank scrape required. Complements post-scrape refresh in Scrape settings.
scheduler.backup_standalone_desc: Runs on its own schedule, independent of scrapes. Uses the same options (daily, weekly, monthly, every N days, or custom cron).
scheduler.backup_desc: Scheduled backups run independently of scrapes.
scheduler.aboutDescription: The scheduler runs on the server. Make sure the container is running during the scheduled time. Notifications will be sent according to your global pipeline settings.

explorer.select_record: Select a scrape record to explore your data
explorer.confirm_exclude: Exclude all future transactions with description: "{{description}}"?
explorer.no_data_available: No transaction data available for this record.
explorer.import_description: Import Excel (XLS/XLSX) or PDF files from banks and credit cards
explorer.drop_files: Drop files here or click to select
explorer.ai_parsing_desc: Use Gemini to extract transactions from complex files
explorer.confirm_delete: Are you sure you want to delete this result?
explorer.ai_categorize_partial: AI categorization failed but cached categories were applied: {{error}}
explorer.select_file_and_query: Please select a file and enter a query
explorer.import_drop_click_hint: or click to select from your computer
explorer.import_provider_name_hint: If set, every imported transaction uses this provider id (overrides detected values).
explorer.import_provider_id_auto: No override (use detected or file values)
explorer.import_same_provider_warning: When importing several files at once, choose files from the same bank or card provider so formats match and results stay consistent.
explorer.import_review_hint: Check and edit the parsed rows, then save them to your library. This step runs for both AI and standard parsing.
explorer.import_account_hint: Choosing an account sets the provider hint and last digits; you can still type a custom number below.
explorer.import_profile_saved_select_label: Saved import formats on this server
explorer.import_profile_saved_empty: No saved import formats yet. Create one from a spreadsheet or load a JSON file.
explorer.import_profile_saved_list_error: Could not load the import format list.
explorer.import_profile_active: Custom tabular import format will be used for Excel files (built-in bank/card format detection is skipped).
explorer.import_profile_hint: Map columns using a local sample file. Only the JSON rules are sent to the server — not your spreadsheet. Share the import format with others without sharing account data.
explorer.import_profile_builder_intro: Open a spreadsheet export on this device, pick the header row and columns, then save. The result is a small JSON file you can reuse or load in Import.
explorer.import_profile_name_hint: A default name is filled from the sheet when you upload a file. It is saved with the import format under data/import_profiles on the server.
explorer.import_profile_sheet_filter_ph: Sheet1, Sheet2 — leave empty for all sheets
explorer.import_profile_test_hint: Runs the same parser as import. Adjust columns and click again to refresh.
explorer.import_profile_test_empty: No transactions matched. Check header row, date format, and column mapping.
explorer.import_profile_test_need_file: Upload a sample spreadsheet first, then run the test.
explorer.import_profile_test_sheet_missing: No sheet is selected or the sheet is missing from the file.
explorer.import_profile_test_sheet_empty: The selected sheet has no rows to read.
explorer.import_profile_test_header_out_of_range: Header row {{row}} is outside the sheet (this sheet has {{total}} row(s)). Lower the header row number or pick another sheet.
explorer.import_profile_test_column_invalid: {{label}}: column index {{index}} is out of range for this sheet. Valid positions are 0 through {{maxIndex}} (widen the table or choose another column).
explorer.import_profile_test_expected_number_got_: Expected a number in "{{label}}", but the cell contains text (not a numeric amount): "{{sample}}". Point this field at the column that has the charged amount as a number.
explorer.import_profile_test_hint_swap_charged: The Charged amount column looks like a currency symbol (e.g. ₪) and Charged currency looks numeric. Swap those two: map Charged amount to the column "סכום חיוב" (charged amount) and Charged currency to "מטבע חיוב" (charged currency). Optionally map Original currency to "מטבע עסקה".
explorer.import_profile_footer_stops: Stop at default Hebrew statement footer rows (totals / future section)
explorer.import_profile_optional_mappings_hint: Add a row per extra column: choose the spreadsheet column, then the Transaction field (memo, voucherNumber, category, status, etc.).
explorer.import_profile_drop_formats: XLSX or CSV, up to 10 MB
explorer.import_profile_file_too_large: File must be 10 MB or smaller.
explorer.import_profile_negate_amounts: Flip amount signs (invert; ×−1 on each value)
explorer.import_profile_negate_amounts_hint: When enabled, each parsed amount is multiplied by −1—the same as flipping its sign (positive becomes negative and vice versa). When off, amounts stay exactly as in the spreadsheet, including mixed positive and negative rows. Turn on only if your file uses the opposite convention from this app (here: negative = expense, positive = income).
explorer.import_profile_amount_filter: Which rows to import by amount sign
explorer.import_profile_amount_filter_hint: Applied after the sign flip above. Rows that do not match are skipped.

analyst.title: AI Analyst
analyst.ask_me: Ask me anything about these {{count}} transactions!

table.search_placeholder_extended: Search by name, memo, category, amount, date…
table.type_meta_filter_label: Filter by transaction type or meta-category
table.export_view_aria: Download current table (filters and sort applied)
table.export_empty: No rows to export for this view
table.reset_all_desc: Deletes the entire server data folder and clears site cookies/storage in this browser. You will lose transactions, profiles, AI memory, OAuth tokens, integrations, local backups, and logs.
table.confirm_reset_all: Are you sure you want to reset all user modifications (categories, filters, ignoring) back to defaults? This cannot be undone.

maintenance.server_paths_subtitle: Same values as runtime-settings.json (PORT, DATA_DIR). Changing the data folder affects where your DB and config live.
maintenance.desktop_app_subtitle: Only shown when you run Financial Overview from the desktop app (not in a normal browser).
maintenance.desktop_app_close_to_tray: Keep the server running when I close the window (close to tray). When enabled, scheduled scrapes and Telegram keep working in the background. Use Quit from the tray menu to stop the server.
maintenance.github_release_subtitle: Compares this build to the latest published release.
maintenance.github_release_up_to_date: You are on the latest release ({{tag}}).
maintenance.github_release_unparsed: Could not compare versions automatically — this build string is not a standard semver. Check the latest release manually.
maintenance.github_release_hint: Uses the public GitHub API (rate limits apply). The build version is set when the app image or installer was built.
maintenance.reload_desc: This will clear all transactions from the unified database and re-import them from the existing JSON files in the results folder.
maintenance.confirm_reload: Are you sure you want to reload the database from files? This will overwrite any unsynced changes.
maintenance.reset_success: All changes have been reset to defaults
maintenance.reset_factory_backup_hint:
Create a backup first (use “Create Local Backup” or “Backup to Google Drive” above). Download or store it outside this app if you need it later.

Continuing will permanently delete everything in the data folder on the server and then clear cookies and site data in this browser.
maintenance.confirm_reset_factory_step1: Have you created a backup if you need one? Click OK to continue to the final confirmation.
maintenance.confirm_factory_reset: This permanently deletes ALL data in the server data folder: transactions, scrape results, bank profiles, AI memory and rules, app lock, OAuth and API settings, Telegram/Google config, local backup files, and logs. This browser tab will clear site storage and reload. This cannot be undone. Continue?
maintenance.reset_factory_success: Server data was wiped and this site’s storage was cleared. Reloading the page…
maintenance.backup_desc: Create local or Google Drive backups and restore from existing snapshots.
maintenance.backup_select_drive_first: Select a Google Drive backup first.
maintenance.backup_confirm_restore_local: Restore from selected local backup? This will replace current data.
maintenance.backup_confirm_restore_drive: Restore from selected Google Drive backup? This will replace current data.
maintenance.backup_confirm_restore_upload: Restore from uploaded backup file? This will replace current data.
maintenance.backup_restore_drive_success: Restore completed from Google Drive backup.
maintenance.backup_restore_upload_success: Restore completed from uploaded backup file.
maintenance.backup_restore_restart_notice: The database was restored. The page will reload to apply changes.
maintenance.backup_scope_restore_label: Restore from backup (applies to all restore actions below)
maintenance.backup_scope_partial_hint: Partial mode: only checked items are written. Partial restore merges into existing data and does not wipe other folders.
maintenance.backup_confirm_restore_partial_local: Restore only the selected parts from this local backup? Matching paths will be overwritten; other data stays as-is.
maintenance.backup_confirm_restore_partial_drive: Restore only the selected parts from this Google Drive backup? Matching paths will be overwritten; other data stays as-is.
maintenance.backup_confirm_restore_partial_upload: Restore only the selected parts from this file? Matching paths will be overwritten; other data stays as-is.
maintenance.backup_scope_config: Configuration folder (integrations, filters, AI, Telegram, Google, etc.)
maintenance.backup_scope_runtime_settings: Runtime settings (API keys, OAuth client id/secret)
maintenance.backup_scope_root_config: Scheduler and notification JSON files (data root)
maintenance.backup_parse_error: This file is not a valid backup snapshot.
maintenance.backup_restore_pick_source: Choose a local backup, a Google Drive backup, or upload a JSON file to see what can be restored.

categorization.banner_detail: {{error}} Cached categories were applied where possible.

transaction_review.banner_title: New transactions need a memo or category
transaction_review.banner_detail: {{count}} new (Transfers or Other). Open the dashboard to add a memo or pick a category.
transaction_review.banner_open_table: Click to open the full list in a table.
transaction_review.modal_subtitle: Add a memo or choose a category for each row. Changes save to your unified data.
transaction_review.modal_empty: No rows to show. Try refreshing the page if data just finished loading.

app_lock.locked_title: Application locked — scraping and saving new profiles are disabled
app_lock.locked_body: Enter your app password below to unlock. Scheduled scrapes, Telegram /scrape, and the Run scrape button stay off until you unlock (the rest of the dashboard stays available).
app_lock.unlocked_hint: Scraping and new profiles are allowed for this session.
app_lock.not_configured_hint: Optional: set an app password to require unlock before bank scrapes or adding profiles.

category_settings.page_description: Default category, allowed labels, meta-grouping, and bulk recategorization.
category_settings.allowed_subtitle: Used when AI assigns a category to transactions.

gemini_card.subtitle: Required for categorization and the AI analyst. Stored in runtime-settings.json.

ai_settings.title: AI Configuration
ai_settings.description: API key, models, quota, and preferences under AI settings; persona & goals plus facts, insights, and alerts under AI memory.
ai_settings.ai_preferences_subtitle: How the analyst should communicate and how deep reports should go. These apply when you use the dashboard AI chat.
ai_settings.models_subtitle: Categorization classifies transactions; Analyst powers the dashboard chat.
ai_settings.fallback_model_none: None — do not retry with another model
ai_settings.fallback_model_help: When Google returns HTTP 429 (rate limit / quota) or 503 (service busy or model overload), the app can retry the same request once using this model. That often succeeds because different models have separate limits and load. Choose a model that is not the same as your primary analyst or categorization model (for example a smaller or alternate Flash tier). This is optional; if you leave it unset, failed calls surface the error as before.
ai_settings.advanced_subtitle: Custom prompts, generation parameters, fallback model, and analyst row cap.
ai_settings.analytics_prompt_extra_label: Extra instructions for analytics & dashboard chat
ai_settings.analytics_prompt_extra_placeholder: e.g. Always mention cash vs. credit separately…
ai_settings.analytics_prompt_extra_help: Appended to every analyst request (dashboard AI chat, legacy file chat, Telegram). The built-in JSON schema for structured replies is unchanged.
ai_settings.analytics_system_extra_placeholder: e.g. Prefer Hebrew labels for categories when listing totals…
ai_settings.analytics_system_extra_help: Appended to the model’s system instruction for both plain and structured analyst calls.
ai_settings.categorization_prompt_extra_placeholder: e.g. Treat all pharmacy chains as Health…
ai_settings.categorization_prompt_extra_help: Inserted into the categorization prompt before the JSON output rules. Your category list above still applies.
ai_settings.categorization_system_extra_placeholder: e.g. Prefer the most specific matching category…
ai_settings.categorization_system_extra_help: Appended to the categorization model’s system instruction.
ai_settings.advanced_gen_defaults_hint: Leave a field empty to use the model default (temperature 0.7 when unset). Optional: topP, topK, max output tokens.
ai_settings.quota_subtitle: Cap how many transaction rows are sent to Gemini on each analyst call (newest rows first). Lower this if you hit rate limits or want smaller prompts.
ai_settings.quota_max_rows_label: Max transaction rows per analyst call
ai_settings.quota_est_intro: Rough input-token estimate for one analyst turn with your current data (CSV + fixed prompt; chat history adds more):
ai_settings.quota_est_note: Figures are approximate. Gemini bills by tokens; limits depend on your project tier.
ai_settings.quota_rate_limits_explainer: Google measures usage in several ways at once: requests per minute (RPM), input tokens per minute (TPM), and requests per day (RPD). Limits apply per project (not per API key). If you exceed any one of these, you get a rate-limit error—even if the others still have headroom. Daily quotas reset at midnight Pacific time. See
ai_settings.quota_free_tier_note: For normal daily use of this app (occasional categorization and analyst questions), the free tier is usually enough. If you scrape huge batches or chat heavily in a short window, you may need to wait, lower the row cap above, or upgrade billing in AI Studio.
ai_settings.quota_link_ai_studio: Your rate limits in AI Studio
ai_settings.quota_ai_logs_hint: See measured prompt, completion, and total tokens for each call in AI Logs (totals and per-model breakdown).
ai_settings.recategorize_all_desc: Without force: runs AI on transactions currently in the default category (skips user-locked descriptions). With force: re-categorizes all non–user-locked descriptions and overwrites existing categories where the model picks a specific label. Uses the allowed categories list above.
ai_settings.recategorize_ai_failed: AI categorization failed: {{error}}. Still updated {{count}} rows from cache where possible.
ai_settings.recategorize_force_requires_ai: Force recategorization needs AI: set GEMINI_API_KEY (or your configured Gemini API key) on the server. Cache is not used for this action.
ai_settings.meta_description: Drag each category into one bucket. Fixed and variable help structure analysis; optimization marks discretionary spend. Income-style categories and transfers belong in “Not in scope”.

persona.card_subtitle: Optional. The financial assistant uses this to match tone, depth, and priorities.
persona.include_in_prompts: Send persona to the AI analyst
persona.include_in_prompts_help: Your answers stay saved below. Turn off the header switch to stop including them in AI prompts.
persona.narrative_notes: Your situation in your own words
persona.narrative_notes_placeholder: Optional: household, jobs, kids, how you use cards…
persona.narrative_notes_help: Optional. Helps the AI understand context; you can refine structured fields below.
persona.section_cards_income_help: Add one row per card or income (e.g. each parent’s salary, a child allowance, separate debit vs credit products).
persona.cards_empty_hint: No cards listed yet — add one per card or person.
persona.incomes_empty_hint: No income streams listed yet — add one per salary, allowance, or pension.
persona.charge_card_payment_day_help: Day of the month this card’s charge is debited (1–31).
persona.card_label_placeholder: Label (optional), e.g. Mom debit, Dad Amex
persona.income_payment_days_help: Tap calendar days when this income usually arrives.
persona.income_label_placeholder: Label (optional), e.g. Salary — Parent A, Child allowance
persona.income_dates_notes_placeholder: e.g. biweekly on the 5th and 20th, variable freelancer payouts…

analytics.no_data: No transactions to analyze. Select a result file first.
analytics.click_hint: Click a chart item to drill down
analytics.merchants_subtitle: Where your money goes most often
analytics.meta_spend_subtitle: Share of expenses in fixed, variable, and optimization (out-of-scope categories are excluded from this chart).
analytics.meta_spend_empty: No expense transactions in this range for the meta breakdown.

google_settings.title: Google Drive Setup
google_settings.description: Obtain these from the Google Cloud Console.
google_settings.redirect_uri_hint: Must match the Authorized redirect URI in Google Console.
google_settings.browseFolderHelp: Browse and select a folder for auto-organization
google_settings.noFoldersInDrive: No folders found in Google Drive

google_sheets.title: Google Sheets
google_sheets.not_configured: Sync to Sheets is not configured yet.
google_sheets.connect_desc: Connect to your Google account to sync transactions.
google_sheets.select_hint: Select a scrape result to sync
google_sheets.connection_error_prompt: Your Google Drive connection has a problem. Connect again to re-authorize.

budget_exports.title: Budget exports
budget_exports.subtitle: Configure outbound sync after each successful scrape. Secrets are stored separately from scrape settings.
budget_exports.load_failed: Failed to load budget export settings.
budget_exports.invalid_account_map_json: Invalid JSON in an account map. Use an object with string keys and string values.
budget_exports.firefly_help: Use a Personal Access Token from your Firefly profile. Map each local account key to a Firefly asset account id (number). Withdrawals use source_id; deposits use destination_id.
budget_exports.lunch_help: Developer token from Lunch Money. Map keys to Lunch Money asset_id (integer).
budget_exports.ynab_help: Register a YNAB OAuth app; redirect URI must match exactly (e.g. https://your-server/api/budget-export/ynab/callback). Then connect and set budget id + account map.
budget_exports.actual_help: Self-hosted Actual server URL, account password, and sync id from the app. Map keys to Actual account ids.

post_scrape.title: Post-Scrape Actions
post_scrape.ignore_pending_desc: Do not enter and delete existing from DB transactions in pending status
post_scrape.fraud_notify_help: Run fraud checks after each scrape. Requires either local rules or AI access.
post_scrape.fraud_notify_toggle: Notify when potential fraud is detected
post_scrape.run_insight_rules_desc: Re-evaluate saved insight rules against your transactions and update dashboard rule matches after each successful scrape.
post_scrape.budget_exports_teaser: Push completed transactions to Firefly III, YNAB, Lunch Money, or Actual Budget after each scrape when enabled.
post_scrape.custom_ai_skip_if_no_tx: Skip when there are no transactions in scope
post_scrape.custom_ai_skip_if_no_tx_desc: When enabled (default), the custom query is not sent if this run has nothing to analyze. Turn off to run the query anyway with an empty list.
post_scrape.telegram_aggregate: Combine Telegram notifications (one message per run)
post_scrape.telegram_aggregate_desc: Merges scrape status, post-scrape insights, and spending digest lines into a single Telegram message when Telegram is enabled.
post_scrape.spending_digest_help: Send a short budget pace, whale, and other alert lines to notification chats after each successful scrape (skipped if nothing changed). Same signals as the dashboard bell.
post_scrape.whale_where: Whale alerts (unusually large single expenses) appear here under Dashboard → bell alerts, and in the combined Telegram message when Spending digest after scrape is enabled above.
post_scrape.review_reminder_title: Remind to set memo or category
post_scrape.review_reminder_desc: For new transactions classified as Transfers or the default category (Other), show a dashboard banner and send alerts on the channels selected below.
post_scrape.notification_help: Choose which channels receive post-scrape messages.

errors.custom_query_required: Custom AI query is required when custom mode is enabled

fraud_settings.title: Fraud & Alerts
fraud_settings.description: Configure local and AI-based fraud detection rules, thresholds, and alerts.
fraud_settings.overview_desc: Choose how fraud is detected after each scrape and when alerts should fire.
fraud_settings.rule_outlier_amount_desc: Flag charges much higher than historical amounts for the same description.
fraud_settings.rule_new_merchant_desc: Flag first-time descriptions above the ₪ threshold, or foreign-currency / Latin-only names even when the amount is small.
fraud_settings.rule_new_merchant_non_hebrew_desc: If the description has Latin letters and no Hebrew, treat as new merchant without the ₪ minimum (ILS charges included).
fraud_settings.rule_rapid_repeats_desc: Detect multiple similar charges on the same day (typical for date-only imports) or within a short time window when times are present.
fraud_settings.rule_foreign_currency_desc: Flag large non-ILS charges that may be unexpected.
fraud_settings.thresholds_intro: Thresholds only control when a rule runs. Points are fixed per rule (see below) and add up to a 0–100 score.
fraud_settings.scores_summary: Score weights: foreign currency +20; new merchant +25 (or configurable points for Latin-only / no-Hebrew names); amount outlier +15–40; repeated similar charges +10–30. Final score is capped at 100.
fraud_settings.outlier_info_p1: We group charges by the same line text (same store or label). If a new charge is much larger than what you usually pay at that place, it counts as an unusual amount for you—not compared to the whole world, only to your own past charges there.
fraud_settings.outlier_info_p2: Min history count means how many older charges we need before we know what “normal” looks like for you. Example: you already have several small grocery bills at “Market ABC”; then a single huge bill at “Market ABC” stands out. If we almost never saw that name before, we can’t compare yet.
fraud_settings.outlier_info_p3: Sensitivity is how big a jump must be before we care. Stricter settings ignore small bumps and only react to very large surprises. Example: you often pay around ₪40 at a café; ₪45 is close enough, but ₪350 on the same line is the kind of surprise this check is for.
fraud_settings.severity_bar_help: Drag M (medium) and H (high) along the bar. Green is low severity, yellow is medium, orange to red is high. Click the bar to jump the nearest handle.
fraud_settings.severity_bar_track_aria: Severity thresholds on a 0–100 score bar
fraud_settings.severity_bar_handle_medium: Drag to set minimum score for medium severity
fraud_settings.severity_bar_handle_high: Drag to set minimum score for high severity
fraud_settings.advanced_severity_low_hint: Low cutoff is kept for compatibility; severity still uses medium and high cutoffs for labels.
fraud_settings.min_new_merchant_help: Minimum ₪ for the standard new-merchant path. New merchants in foreign currency or Latin-only descriptions bypass this floor.
fraud_settings.non_hebrew_points_help: Points when the new-merchant rule applies to a Latin-only description (no Hebrew letters). Default 35. Other new-merchant cases use +25.
fraud_settings.min_fx_amount_help: Minimum original (foreign) amount before the foreign-currency rule adds +20 points.
fraud_settings.rapid_window_help: Peers count if they are on the same calendar day (works when dates are midnight-only) or their timestamps are within this many minutes.
fraud_settings.rapid_count_help: How many similar charges (same description, amount within 5%) must appear in the same scrape batch. Peers match on the same calendar day or within a fixed minute span (default 30; not editable here).
fraud_settings.outlier_history_help: How many past transactions with the same description are required before z-score outlier detection runs.
fraud_settings.outlier_zscore_help: How many standard deviations above the historical mean counts as an outlier; higher = stricter.
fraud_settings.sev_medium_help: Minimum total score to label a finding as medium severity.
fraud_settings.sev_high_help: Minimum total score to label a finding as high severity.
fraud_settings.notify_severity_help: When “notify on issue” is on, only findings at or above this severity trigger notifications.
fraud_settings.persist_only_flagged_help: If enabled, only transactions with a score above zero are stored as fraud findings.
fraud_settings.version_help: Bumped into finding IDs; change when scoring rules change so records stay consistent.
fraud_settings.test_intro: Simulate local detection with the settings above (saved or not). No data is stored.
fraud_settings.test_history_invalid: History must be valid JSON array
fraud_settings.test_would_notify: Would notify at current “notify from severity” setting.
fraud_settings.test_would_not_notify: Would not notify at current “notify from severity” setting.

dashboard.title: Financial Command Center
dashboard.select_data: Select transaction files to view your financial dashboard
dashboard.select_hint: Choose files from the sidebar to start analyzing
dashboard.search_all_transactions_subtitle: {{count}} transactions loaded — use search and the same filters as the monthly table
dashboard.recurring_hint: Recurring items are detected from your transaction history
dashboard.transfers_excluded: {{amount}} excluded from expense totals to prevent double-counting
dashboard.transfer_explanation: These are credit card settlement payments from your bank account. The individual card transactions are already counted as expenses, so these lump-sum payments are excluded to avoid double-counting.
dashboard.custom_charts_intro: Build charts from your transaction data. Settings are saved with your dashboard.
dashboard.custom_charts_none: No custom charts yet. Add one to track spending, income, or counts the way you want.
dashboard.custom_charts_empty_data: No data for this selection in the current scope.
dashboard.custom_charts_limit: You can save up to {{max}} custom charts. Remove one to add another.
dashboard.custom_charts_pie_net_hint: Pie charts are not available for net balance (values can be negative).
dashboard.custom_charts_filters_scope_hint: Filters apply on top of the data period you choose below (all conditions must match).
dashboard.custom_charts_data_scope_hint: Choose which transactions feed this chart: match the analytics toggle, all loaded data, a calendar month, rolling windows, or a custom date range.
dashboard.custom_charts_scope_follow: Same as analytics (follows This month / All months above)
dashboard.custom_charts_scope_single_month: Single calendar month (from all loaded data)
dashboard.custom_charts_scope_last_n_days: Last N days (rolling, from all loaded data)
dashboard.custom_charts_scope_last_n_months: Last N calendar months (rolling, from all loaded data)
dashboard.custom_charts_scope_custom: Custom date range (from all loaded data)
dashboard.custom_charts_last_n_days_hint: Includes today and the previous N−1 days (local calendar).
dashboard.custom_charts_last_n_months_hint: Includes this month and the previous N−1 calendar months.
dashboard.custom_charts_last_n_days_invalid: Enter a number of days between 1 and {{max}}.
dashboard.custom_charts_last_n_months_invalid: Enter a number of months between 1 and {{max}}.
dashboard.custom_charts_custom_range_invalid: Please choose a valid date range (from ≤ to).
dashboard.custom_charts_filter_categories_placehol: Comma or newline separated category names
dashboard.custom_charts_filter_categories_hint: Use the same labels as in your category list / AI settings.
dashboard.no_txns_month: No transactions found for this month

budget_health_msg.pace_much_faster: Spending much faster than historical average.

velocity.msg: Spending in {{category}} is ahead of your usual pace for this point in the month.

outlier.msg: {{category}} is trending well above your typical monthly level.

whale.msg_with_avg: One expense of {{amount}} is much larger than your typical {{category}} charge (~{{avgAmount}}).
whale.msg_no_avg: One expense of {{amount}} stands out in {{category}} this month.

dashboard.cc_billing_desc: Set the day of the month your credit cards are cleared. Used for more accurate projections.
dashboard.message_spending_much_faster_than_histor: Spending much faster than historical average.
dashboard.forecast_calculation: Based on current daily pace and historical baseline
dashboard.ask_ai_placeholder: Ask about your spending habits, trends or specific anomalies...
dashboard.ai_disclaimer: AI can make mistakes. Please verify important financial information.
dashboard.subscription_hint: Mark regular transactions as subscriptions in their details modal.
dashboard.no_history_details: No detailed history available for this item
dashboard.forecast_details_modal_hint: Open for transaction pace vs. monthly average and full calculation
dashboard.no_transactions: No transactions found for this calculation.
dashboard.forecast_window_desc: Months of data used to compute spending averages. Capped to your available data.
dashboard.cc_patterns_desc: Transactions flagged as CC payments. Remove to stop auto-detection.
dashboard.method_transaction_count_detail: {{remainingDays}} days left in the month. The forecast expects {{forecastTxnCount}} more transactions. At ~{{avgTxnValue}} ₪ per transaction.
dashboard.forecast_explanation: Extra variable spending projected for the next {{count}} days, beyond bills we already detected.
dashboard.forecast_how_body: Only for the current calendar month: for each variable category we use your last {{months}} month(s) of history. We estimate N (typical expense txns/month) and average debit per txn. The raw gap is (N − current txns) × avg per txn, but we cap how many extra txns we assume by the share of the month left: at most N × (days left ÷ days in month). We take the lower of the gap and that cap, then multiply by avg per txn. If you already reached N txns, we add nothing. Categories without a baseline use (spent so far ÷ days elapsed) × days left. The total is only the portion above known upcoming bills per category.
dashboard.txn_pace_modal_current: {{current}} expense transactions so far vs ~{{expected}} expected by today (pro‑rated from your long‑term average of {{avg}} transactions per month).
dashboard.txn_pace_modal_past: {{current}} expense transactions this month · historical average {{avg}} transactions per month.
dashboard.forecast_disclaimer: Estimates only; they change as new transactions arrive.
dashboard.top_insights_subtitle: AI analyst and insight rules, by importance
dashboard.top_insights_empty: Use the AI analyst chat to add scored insights—they appear here by importance.
dashboard.no_transactions_month: No transactions found for this month
dashboard.forecast_extra_vs_bills: Extra forecast vs. known upcoming bills
dashboard.subscriptions_logic_intro: Subscriptions are detected from transaction behavior and your manual overrides:
dashboard.subscriptions_logic_rule_manual: Manual mark in transaction details always wins and sets the interval.
dashboard.subscriptions_logic_rule_pattern: Auto-detection requires repeating timing with similar amounts and merchant text.
dashboard.subscriptions_logic_rule_exclusions: Ignored/internal-transfer/installment transactions and excluded items are skipped.
dashboard.subscriptions_logic_rule_active: Old patterns are hidden when they look inactive for their billing interval.
dashboard.detectionAnalyzesHistory: Analyzes your entire transaction history to find consistent patterns:
dashboard.detectionRuleDescriptionMultiMonth: Description appears in multiple months (3 of last 4)
dashboard.detectionRuleEstimatedDate: Estimated date based on past occurrences (+/-3 days)

ai_chat.greeting: Hello! I am your AI financial analyst. I can help answer questions about your spending habits, trends, or anomalies. What would you like to know?
ai_chat.error: Sorry — I hit an error while analyzing your data. Please try again.
ai_chat.fallback_used: This reply used your fallback model ({{model}}) after a temporary rate limit or overload on the primary model.

model_high_demand.title: AI model is busy
model_high_demand.description: This model is currently experiencing high demand. Spikes in demand are usually temporary.
model_high_demand.solution: Please try again in a few moments.

ai_memory.title: AI memory
ai_memory.description: Facts are long-term context the model can reuse (editable). Insights are analytical notes stored so the assistant avoids repeating the same observation.
ai_memory.retention_help: Automatically delete insights and alerts older than the number of days below (0 = keep forever). Pruning runs on save, daily, and when the server starts.
ai_memory.clear_all_confirm: Remove every item in “{{section}}”? This cannot be undone.
ai_memory.facts_help: Examples: goals, household situation, preferences. The AI may suggest new facts; you can edit or delete them here.
ai_memory.insights_help: Observations from past replies. Delete items if you want them to be allowed again in future answers.
ai_memory.alerts_help: Urgent or time-sensitive items produced by the assistant and stored here (scores are 1–100). Dismissing removes the row and prevents the same text from being saved again.

insight_rules.heading: Insight rules
insight_rules.subtitle: Deterministic rules over your transactions produce scored insights in English and Hebrew. They merge with AI chat insights on the dashboard.
insight_rules.help: Build rules with If / Then, or edit the v1 JSON under Advanced. Message placeholders fill in when a rule matches — open the Engine reference inside the rule form when editing.
insight_rules.open_builder_hint: To use the If / Then builder, expand “Add rule” under the list (or choose Edit on a rule). The form—including optional AI draft—opens inside that section.
insight_rules.name: Rule name
insight_rules.priority_badge_title: This rule’s priority is {{n}} (shown as p{{n}}). Higher numbers are listed first in the rules list.
insight_rules.advanced_json_hint: Edit the raw v1 definition. Save uses this text when it differs from the form (or when the visual builder is unavailable).
insight_rules.builder_incompatible: This rule uses conditions the visual builder does not support (for example nested NOT/OR). Edit JSON below or simplify the rule to use the form.
insight_rules.create_with_ai: Optional: get a draft from AI
insight_rules.import_review_hint: Adjust categories, amounts, and percentages to match your data before saving. Message text ({{placeholders}}) is unchanged.
insight_rules.import_review_no_slots: No category or numeric fields were found to customize; you can import as-is.
insight_rules.share_mask_amounts: Mask amounts in rule JSON and in this description (“X”)
insight_rules.copy_mask_amounts: Mask numeric thresholds when copying (“X” in JSON)
insight_rules.save_needs_conditions: Add at least one complete IF condition, or edit Advanced JSON.
insight_rules.ai_draft_hint: Describe the rule in plain language (English or Hebrew). AI returns a JSON definition you can edit before saving.
insight_rules.ai_draft_prompt_placeholder: e.g. Alert when food spending this month exceeds 3000 NIS
insight_rules.strategy_placeholder: Short note on what this rule is for (stored with the rule).
insight_rules.param_net_savings_lte: Net savings (income − expenses) at or below
insight_rules.net_savings_help: Net savings = sum of income minus sum of expenses in the scope. Alert when this net is less than or equal to the value (negative means you spent more than you earned).
insight_rules.engine_reference_intro: Use the If / Then builder for common cases, or paste JSON in Advanced. Income conditions treat positive transaction amounts as income (non-ignored).
insight_rules.engine_reference_conditions_hint: Combine leaves with and / or / not. Per-transaction checks use existsTxn and a where tree.
insight_rules.ph_desc_sum: Expense total for the focused category (or all expenses), formatted.
insight_rules.ph_desc_count: Number of expense transactions in that slice.
insight_rules.ph_desc_category: Category taken from the rule when present, otherwise the top expense category in scope.
insight_rules.ph_desc_sum_raw: Same total as sum without thousands separators.
insight_rules.ph_desc_threshold: First threshold from the condition tree (amounts, counts, or %).
insight_rules.ph_desc_avg_txn: Average expense amount (sum ÷ count) when count is positive.
insight_rules.ph_desc_pct_of_total: Category expenses as % of all expenses (empty if no category focus).
insight_rules.ph_desc_top_memo: Memo/description with the highest expense total in the slice.
insight_rules.ph_desc_largest_txn_amount: Largest single expense amount in the slice.
insight_rules.ph_desc_dominant_account: Account with the highest expense total in the slice.
insight_rules.ph_desc_delta_prior: Change in category (or all) expense total vs the previous period (+/−). Empty for “all time” scope.
insight_rules.ph_desc_preview_3: Up to three largest expenses as short lines (date, memo, amount).
insight_rules.ph_desc_period_label: Human-readable period for the rule scope (localized per message language).
insight_rules.summary_share: category “{{cat}}” is at least {{pct}}% of all expenses
insight_rules.message_placeholder_hint: Use placeholders like sum, count, period_label, top_memo in double braces (see Engine reference).
insight_rules.summary_no_conditions: No valid conditions yet — add and complete at least one.
insight_rules.summary_test_hint: Use “Test rule” after saving to see if current data matches.
insight_rules.community_help: Share a rule through this app’s API (server → Google Apps Script → GitHub), or import rules from the public catalog. The catalog list is cached in this browser and refreshes in the background when you open this section.
insight_rules.community_defaults_active_hint: Official catalog (raw GitHub) URLs are built in. Open Advanced settings only to use a mirror or fork of the community repo.
insight_rules.community_advanced_help: Leave a field empty to use the official default. Your overrides are saved in this browser only.
insight_rules.community_reset_official_urls: Reset catalog & script URLs to official defaults
insight_rules.community_proxy_enabled: Submit goes through this app’s API to Google Apps Script (no browser CORS). GAS URL is built into the server; only the shared secret is configured on the server.
insight_rules.community_proxy_required_hint:
Sharing is disabled until the backend has COMMUNITY_INSIGHT_RULES_SECRET set (same value as AUTH_SECRET in Apps Script).

Add it under your data folder, e.g. server/data/config/runtime-settings.json:
"COMMUNITY_INSIGHT_RULES_SECRET": "<your-secret>"

Then restart the server. Optional: override the script URL with COMMUNITY_INSIGHT_RULES_GAS_URL.
insight_rules.community_gas_url: Google Apps Script web app URL
insight_rules.community_catalog_description_hint: Shown in the community index. You can edit the text below; clear it to regenerate from the rule.
insight_rules.community_index_url_required: Set the catalog index URL in settings (or expand Connection settings).
insight_rules.community_raw_base_required: Set the raw content base URL so rule files can be downloaded.
insight_rules.community_import_modal_hint: Same editor as when you edit a rule: adjust name, conditions, or JSON, then save to add a copy to your rules.

telegram.status_inactive_subtitle: Start the bot to enable Telegram commands and notifications.
telegram.token_help: Get your token from @BotFather on Telegram
telegram.send_test_to_user: Send test message to this chat
telegram.users_management_help: Add Telegram user IDs, then set allowed chat and notifications per row.
telegram.accounts_routing_help: Comma-separated account numbers for per-transaction memo prompts in Telegram. Use * for all accounts. Accounts not listed on any row still notify every notification chat.
telegram.token_security_line1: Bot token is hidden while the bot is running.
telegram.token_security_line2: to edit or view the security token.
telegram.why_bot_not_started: Why the bot did not start
telegram.chat_id_placeholder: Enter chat ID or group ID
telegram.chat_id_help: Typically starts with - for groups or is a number for private chats
telegram.notification_chats_help: Select which chats should receive transaction notifications
telegram.allowed_users_help: Only listed Telegram user IDs can use the bot.
telegram.bot_language_help: Select the primary communication language for automated fiscal reports and alerts.
telegram.warning_no_users_desc: The bot will reject all commands until at least one user is added.
telegram.bot_identity_unavailable: Could not load bot details. Check that the token is valid.
telegram.bot_identity_error: Could not reach Telegram to load bot details.

transaction_modal.aria_toggle_ignore: Toggle ignored state for this transaction
transaction_modal.aria_toggle_subscription: Toggle subscription flag for this transaction
transaction_modal.aria_toggle_exclude_subscriptions: Toggle exclusion from automatic subscription detection

onboarding.footer_hint: You can close this window anytime and resume from the banner below. Nothing is required to use the app — every step is optional.
onboarding.save_failed: Could not save. Try again or skip this step.
onboarding.open_ai_studio: Open Google AI Studio (get API key)
onboarding.redirect_uri: Authorized redirect URI (must match Google Cloud)
onboarding.leave_blank_unchanged: Leave blank if you already saved a secret
onboarding.lock_already_configured: An app password is already set. You can continue to the next step or change it later under the lock banner.
onboarding.restart_recommended: You saved connection settings. Restart the server so the backend loads them.
onboarding.gemini_assistant_after_save: After you tap Next and save, App Assistant opens automatically (blue bot in the header). You can ask how to use the app without restarting the server. When you finish the whole setup, restart once so every AI feature can load your key.
onboarding.restart_after_setup_title: Restart the server when you finish setup
onboarding.restart_after_setup_body: App Assistant (help chat) works as soon as your key is saved. For the full AI experience — automatic categorization, the dashboard analyst, persona extraction, and anything else that loads Gemini when the server starts — restart the server once after you close this wizard. Use the button below or Maintenance → Restart when you are ready.
onboarding.done_next_scrape: Run a scrape from the Scrape tab to import transactions.
onboarding.done_next_config: Open Configuration anytime to tune AI, Telegram, Sheets, and more.
onboarding.done_next_telegram: Optional: add Telegram in Configuration → Telegram for notifications.
onboarding.resume_banner: Setup paused at step {{step}}. Continue when you are ready.
onboarding.rerun_confirm: Re-run the setup wizard from the beginning?
onboarding.persona_prompt_body: You added a Gemini API key. A short optional survey helps align tone, goals, and reporting with your preferences.
onboarding.persona_narrative_placeholder: Example: Two parents and one child; two salaries around the 1st and 10th; debit card plus a charge card paid on the 2nd…
onboarding.persona_narrative_hint: Write freely in any language. You can extract structured fields with AI, then adjust them on the next step.
onboarding.persona_extract_next_hint: Continue to the next step to review and edit all fields before saving.
onboarding.persona_extract_failed: Could not extract facts. Check your Gemini key and try again.
onboarding.persona_setup_after_restart_hint: Your Gemini API key is active on the server. Optionally personalize how the AI assistant and analyst talk to you — you can change this anytime under Configuration → AI.

steps.drive_title: Default Drive folder for optional backup
steps.welcome_tip: Tip: use the EN / HE button in the header to switch language anytime.
steps.step_0_body:
This app runs on your computer. Your bank data stays local unless you choose to sync with Google.

We will walk you through optional settings: securing saved profiles, AI (Gemini), Telegram notifications, and more. You can skip anything and finish later. After you save a Gemini key and restart the server, a separate optional step helps you personalize the AI persona. Google Drive, Sheets, and OAuth are under Configuration → Google.
steps.step_1_body:
If you save bank profiles in the app, they are encrypted with a password you choose. This password is not sent to the cloud — it only unlocks your saved credentials on this machine.

Minimum 8 characters. You can skip and enable the lock later from the banner.
steps.step_2_body:
Optional Telegram bot notifications. Paste a bot token from @BotFather, or add it later under Configuration → Telegram along with allowed user IDs.

Skip this step if you do not use Telegram.
steps.step_3_body:
Gemini powers automatic categorization, the dashboard AI analyst, and more. Without a key, those features stay off; scraping and viewing data still work.

Get a free API key from Google AI Studio and paste it below. After you save, you can use App Assistant (help) right away; restart the server once at the end of setup for every other AI feature.
steps.step_persona_about_body: In your own words, describe your household, cards, incomes, and anything that helps the assistant understand you. You can use “Extract facts with AI” to fill structured fields, then review everything on the next step.
steps.step_persona_body: Optional: review dropdowns, cards, incomes, and goals. This shapes tone and focus in the dashboard AI chat. You can change it anytime under Configuration → AI.
steps.step_4_body:
To connect Google Sheets and related features, the app needs OAuth credentials from Google Cloud: a Client ID and Client Secret. Add the redirect URI below to your OAuth client in Google Cloud — it must match exactly.

You can skip this and add Google later under Configuration (including optional Drive backup folder there).
steps.step_5_body: Optional backup: if you use a specific Google Drive folder for backups or exports, paste its folder ID here (the long part of the URL after /folders/). Leave empty to skip — you can pick a folder in the app later.
steps.step_6_body: That is it for the guided setup. If you changed OAuth or other connection settings, restart the server once so the backend picks them up. For Gemini, also restart once after you finish so categorization, analyst, and related features use your key everywhere — App Assistant already works from the moment you saved the key.

getting_started.resume_banner: App tour paused at step {{step}}. Resume when you are ready.
getting_started.footer_hint: Use the top tabs to switch areas anytime. Help Docs in the header stay available; App Assistant appears after you configure a Gemini API key. The indigo floating button opens the AI Financial Analyst from any tab. You can close this tour and resume from the banner below.
getting_started.rerun_confirm: Start the app tour again from the beginning?
getting_started.step_0_body:
This short tour walks you through the main workflow: save a bank profile, run your first scrape, then explore the dashboard, logs, and configuration.

Nothing here is required to continue — follow along when you are ready.

After you configure a Gemini API key (Configuration → AI), App Assistant (blue bot icon) appears in the top-right next to the language switch. Help Docs (question mark icon) opens the full bilingual guide (GUIDE.html) in a new tab.
getting_started.step_1_body:
Open the Scrape tab (we switch there for you). In the form at the top, choose your bank and sign in, or pick a saved profile if you already created one.

If app lock is enabled, unlock when prompted so the app can use saved credentials. Use the gear next to the form for advanced scraper options.
getting_started.step_2_body:
Still on Scrape: start a run with your profile. Watch live progress, then review transactions in the results area below.

After a successful run, your data feeds the dashboard and explorer.
getting_started.step_3_body:
The Dashboard tab summarizes income and spending by month, categories, subscriptions, and trends. Pick a month from the selector and drill into charts and lists.

This is the best place for a high-level picture of your finances.

For AI questions about your own transactions and trends, tap the floating indigo button at the bottom-right—it is available on every main tab, not only here (set up Gemini under Configuration → AI first). That is separate from App Assistant in the header, which answers how to use the app and opens from the blue bot icon.
getting_started.step_4_body:
The Logs tab shows server, client, AI, and scrape activity. Use it to troubleshoot failed scrapes, API issues, or automation.

Switch log types with the tabs at the top and inspect entries when something goes wrong.
getting_started.step_5_body:
Configuration holds AI, memory, scheduler, scraper (and fraud), Google (OAuth & Sheets), Telegram, and maintenance (backups, port, data folder).

We open the Scraper section first — tune defaults here; other tabs cover integrations and automation.

env.title: Environment Variables
env.subtitle: Configure system-level settings stored in data/config/runtime-settings.json (next to your database; persists with the data folder / Docker volume)
env.restart_required_hint: Changes to these variables require a server restart to take effect.
env.confirm_restart_after_save: Settings saved. Restart server now to apply?
env.restart_in_progress: Server is restarting. Please refresh the page in a few seconds.

google_client_secret.label: Google Client Secret
google_client_secret.help: Found in the same Google Cloud Console project.

google_redirect_uri.label: Google Redirect URI
google_redirect_uri.help: Must match the authorized redirect URI in Cloud Console (e.g., {{example}})

drive_folder_id.label: Drive Folder ID
drive_folder_id.help: The ID from the URL of your Google Drive folder where results will be saved.

data_dir.label: Data Directory
data_dir.help: Path where profiles and results are stored. Default is ./data

help.title: App Assistant
help.empty_state: Ask me anything about how to use the app!

feedback.open_aria: Open feedback form (issues, features, general feedback)
feedback.chat_link: Report an issue or send feedback
feedback.modal_intro: We will open the feedback form in a new tab. Installation and version are shown here for reference; paste them into the form if you like. Optional logs are only loaded if you opt in below (copy and paste into the form).
feedback.consent_server: I agree to attach the latest server log excerpt to this report (last lines only).
feedback.consent_client: I agree to attach the latest error log excerpt to this report (last lines only).
feedback.demo_no_logs: Demo mode has no real server or error logs to attach.
feedback.form_not_configured: The feedback form URL is missing. Edit client/src/config/feedbackGoogleForm.ts.
feedback.log_fetch_failed: Could not load logs. You can still open the form without logs.
feedback.copy_hint: If you included logs, copy the excerpt below and paste it into the form (e.g. the issue report field).
